title: Perpetual Next USA to Develop Three Biomethanol Facilities in Southeastern U.S.
content: Perpetual Next USA has announced plans to develop three new biomass-to-biomethanol production facilities in the Southeastern United States, with development commencing in 2026. Each facility is designed to process approximately one million tons of sustainably sourced biomass annually. This expansion aims to meet the surging global demand for low-carbon fuels driven by initiatives like Europeâ€™s FuelEU program. The company is deploying its proprietary "Biomethanol Blueprint," which integrates torrefaction, gasification, and methanol synthesis to facilitate rapid scaling. CEO Rene Buwalda highlighted the Southeastern U.S. as a strategic location due to its strong feedstock availability and infrastructure. To support this rollout, Perpetual Next is collaborating with U.S. partners, including TSI for feedstock handling and Fluor as Ownerâ€™s Engineer, advancing biomethanol as a scalable solution for shipping and industry.
author: Analysts Account
